body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

list but if people have opinions that want to express now is a good time to do so and I'll also point out there is some overlap with IDM potency key and for example the braid work that's being taken up in each to Deb working group and um some other things like that so uh I I do foresee a little bit of a little bit more scope creep coming up um just to put that on the radar okay um did you say braid or how do you spell which yeah uh just braid okay um because that deals with version numbers and things and uh yeah um hi everybody um I'm a bit surprised to hear that stripe used an intermediary approach to implement your right imp potency as I would have imagined that Financial transactions require you to implement item potency at a much more fine or ingrained level in your application logic but if that's working that's great for them and overall I think for many applications that don't handle such critical transactions implementing item potency via an intermediary is a really viable and practical solution so I would be in favor of including those Solutions into the draw um I think that's uh would be a a great change to allow also those implementations or to consider them that's just my point on on this question so you are in favor of including them or or or against including them I am in favor of including those in the scope okay [Music] so taking chair hat off um I think a a big chunk of the feedback on this spec was around um the fact that it didn't really Define an interoperable solution it require it relied very heavily on pre understanding between client and server uh as to how these things were going to work um and um we it expanded Scope when we introduced the idea of um being able to have a client just arbitrarily send it even if it doesn't know whether the server or not can um support item potency and I think going even further and trying to to enable this middleware type solution um just requires a whole bunch of knowledge about the back end like I'm creating a resource do I have to provide an identifier for it if the back end assumes that it's going to provide its own identifier that's going to be quite difficult uh for an intermediary to know which way around that goes so I just I I think we're just going to get a lot more scrope creep and we're not going to be able to achieve uh our original objective of trying to produce something that will enable interoperable Solutions if we just keep adding more capability so I would be a vote to know not to uh try and solve this intermediate service problem thank you all right um anyone else have comments or on my interpretation of the GitHub issue all right what we'll do is we will post a summary and a pointer to the mailing list and allow a week or two couple weeks to conclude and then we'll make a consensus call as to what the working group you know wants to support thank you all uh next slide uh okay Austin do you want to say some things about bite range yes I would be happy to um bite range patch as you probably know it's a media type just to um write to specific ranges of an existing file or maybe even create a new one it's sort of the inverse of a partial get um so you should be able to do a partial patch and then do a partial get and get that back the same contents um I posted a draft before the deadline this time with um some changes mostly around how you do indeterminate length uploads so um that sort of clarifies that um the upload is not considered complete by the server until it knows the final length of the document um because the client doesn't necessarily have to provide the complete length at the beginning um so you can provide that complete length at the end if you're streaming an unknown length document once you hit the end then you can tell the server by the way the end is the last bite I just uploaded um and I also have a a web browser in server implementation um which may be handy in some tests um which I have to publish so I hoped to get that done a little bit sooner but um also I saw Thea considerations um com so I have a revision that can go out just changing the title of that section heading um and other than that just a few questions and things um I don't particularly like the the name of the transaction preference or the values for that so um if anyone would like to bik shed some other ideas for that that would be fantastic um and general ideas for if this integrates well with resumable uploads and uh braid and other um ideas in the going around in the HTTP environment right now any questions Mike Bishop um so I saw on the mailing list there was some discussion of uh other units other than bytes for this MH um and I wasn't deeply involved in the pieces of that but um I can say that in previous discussion in the HTP working group we have attempted to use other units than bytes and they typically don't work very well with deployed servers so I like your direction of match the syntax so that other units might be used but in practice it will almost always be bites um if you could drive new usage that would be interesting I will say that one one area where we have wanted to use a unit other than bytes in the past is with content encoding where um a get request arrange request for something that is say gzip is doing bites inside the gzip representation and some sometimes people would like to be able to retrieve bite off sets of the uncompressed thing and there's not really a way to do that in the HTTP space so I suspect patch is not going to work very well with um with gzip anyway so you're probably acting on the on the actual Resource as it is that may not be but that's actually an interesting point I've never thought of that before yeah um thank you Marius yes um I don't want to repeat my comment from the mailing list regarding the range units but just want to point out if the draft continues supporting different range units it might be worth renaming it right because that's not for bites only anymore but maybe a bit more generic name yeah um that is open to change actually I was also just thinking um like the name of the draft could even be renamed to emphasize its uses rather than how it's being implemented um you know maybe rename it to partial rights or something for sure okay so your plan is to post a new draft at some point and then post maybe post your questions to the list would you do that yeah okay thank you thank you so that last issue on this slide was just a call out uh that we got a early review from Seer of the authentication link uh the repo in GitHub for authentication link has no no open issues on it so uh if you have opinions on it that's where you go open issues yeah we should put the seia review Into the issues list review from him um interestingly I was the next one on the Queue and terara decided not to give me the draft okay okay so that brings us to our next slide uh link hints there are a number of open issues on this uh although I don't believe I saw a whole lot of activity um is Mark around to comment on progress uh no I don't believe I believe he's elsewhere okay okay well then moving on uh so this brings us to um the media type registration um for uh the open API media type and Json schema um we are trying to move the uh rest API one uh forward some work has been done on that for the uh open API specification the blocking issue with regards to uh security considerations has been resolved uh I believe uh this draft is currently expired we need to get an update to that um but I don't believe there are any real blocking issues other than possibly um thoughts around suffixes and if any of you were in the meeting the other day uh media man about suffixes there is some question about the future of suffixes so it's kind of putting up in the air whether or not we should register an open API plus Jason and an open API plus yamal and whether we should have an open API that doesn't have a suffix uh and whilst there is some uncertainty around the future suffixes it does beg the question as to what is the best step forward with the open API registration the Jason schema one is is bogged down in a bunch of other more challenging issues so uh we're gonna try and get the open API one out first okay um so what are you going to do about the open API and the suffixes do you know yet no and I'm not sure waiting for suffixes to be resolved is the right solution either because that's been a little bit of an ongoing issue um but if anybody has any opinions as to whether or not they think there should be a application slopen API without suffix or whether we should only have things with suffix I would love to hear opinions okay okay moving on uh yes um removing chair hat uh I have taken over from Roberto uh on the rate limit headers I unfortunately unlike Austin didn't manage to get a draft in in time uh I have perform done a a significant number of updates that are currently in a PR to address uh comments from previous um uh call meetings uh there is now a quot a unit property um we now have a way of specifying a scope um the scope property is currently called a partition key with the concept that whenever you define some kind of quota policy um there is some kind of partition of how much um that quota is available depending on who the client is what resource they're accessing who the user is who's using that um uh using that particular application uh the real this is quite a common pattern on the server side for doing rate limiting where some kind of key will be constructed in order to be able to uniquely track the usage and what is remaining uh what has never existed in the past is um a way of communicating that key back down to the client so that the client can predictively say oh I'm about to make a request that has this particular partition key and therefore I know how much is remaining for this particular partition key um previously um the idea of using scope had or actually implementing in the spec the notion of scope had been kind of put off for a future or it was well we'll we'll let people use extensions to do that um but based on a bunch of conversations I think that might be one of the reasons why we don't see a lot of generic implementations because clients can't do anything predictively in order to limit requests um because they have no idea whether it's going to be R limited or not because they don't know which partition a particular request falls into so um hopefully I will finish writing that up fairly soon and I would really appreciate feedback around there I think the biggest question in my mind is this partition key is made up of a bunch of different values and I don't know how to communicate those set of values to a client in the right way so that's what I'm going to be looking for a bunch of feedback on can I ask for a fortnite unit uh the the the latest proposal is is a registry so there would be a registry of quota units so okay okay finish my excuses for rate limiting moving on to somebody who's done some work Marius um yes I guess I have to stop sharing right yes there we go I have sto sharing and I believe you can now share your there you go yeah okay um yeah thank you very much this is a brief uh presentation of some work regarding problem types for digest fields that we do together with Lucas partu and also quite some involvement from Roberto poy um although he's not here today to briefly freshen up um digest Fields is a pretty recent RFC that allows um especially requests to include Integrity fields that then contain one or multiple digests in theory this works on any message so also responses but for this work we're particularly looking at requests um um there's the representation digest header for representation data and content digest for the message content and on the right hand side we have one example where a client might send a put request and along the request content it also includes the representation digest which is a sh 256 of the message content or the representation um and this is pretty handy the server can valid ated um they can make sure that they're talking about the same representation but whenever there's a problem arises with this for example let's assume the digest doesn't match the representation that the server receives there is no standardized way of reporting this problem back to the client um and this was intentionally left out of the RFC um because it didn't want to it just want to specify a method of communicating digest but not how to handle those um however if uh a server now wants to implement this there's no really standardized way of reporting those problems back if they want to use a standardized approach luckily we nowadays also have um the problem details RFC um or a new version thereof which provides a machinable machine readable description of problems that can either be encoded in Json or XML one example response we see on the right hand side um on in bold we see uh the custom content type that's used for this um it's adjacent object in this case includes a few interesting details um there's the type which is a unique identifier for the problem at hand for example in this case it's about uh an entity being out of credit um also includes a title and additional details like what was the balance that was requested stuff like this um so really handy format and this is also um applicable to our situation so we can use those problem details for communicating problems related to Integrity checking um so for example on the left hand side we have a request it's similar as before we see a put request but uh in the content you can see uh there's a few bold values indicating that the content was changed somehow by somebody some entity and now the content doesn't match the representation digest anymore and then the server hopefully detects this and can then respond with a problem detail um we have a type um for now that we propose um about a mismatching digest value and we can then use the additional members of a problem detail to provide more context of this problem so what was the algorithm that was used because in theory there may be multiple digest in the request uh what was the digest that the request provided but what was the digest that the server calculated it's can be really handy for debugging to figure out okay what was the actual value that the server received or computed it digest on um so yeah this is an example response um this is just one uh of the problem details that we have thought of um but overall with this draft the idea was to define a small number of highlevel problem types um so for now we thought about mismatching digest values about um reporting that the server might not support an algorithm that the client requested or maybe about an invalid digest value so for example the digest uh generated by Sha 512 always has like a specific length if the length doesn't match you probably don't have a sha 512 um there might also be other problem details some of those might not be um specific enough to deserve a own um problem type but the general idea is that those are just building blocks for other work either inside the ITF or outside people can then use them um people can require them to be used in specific cases we don't want to uh force people to use digest in a specific way we just want to provide um a set of problem details that they can use if they want to use Integrity fields in their applications there might also be um some interest in defining problem types for HTTP signatures because it touches on similar topics with algorithms and signatures not matching so maybe that's also a sensible extension um but we'll see where this goes um but yeah this is uh the idea we had uh and now there's the question to the working group um whether this is something of interest that this working group would like to consider um or if there's any other feedback on this topic so you also presented this at HTTP right I didn't present it there I just uh posted on a mailing list okay um but there was no dedicated presentation all right I just wondered um we can avoid stepping on each other's toes but I just wondered if you know if if they were clamoring and said yeah yeah yeah we want to do this then we'd let them do it but if you know it hasn't been you have a preference as to where you want to see this um it's a pretty good point I thought about this as well um the digest Fields is a product of the HTP Bas working group if I recall correctly um but this feels to touch a bit more on the API side so I can imagine this belonging into the API working group bit more okay so just couple of questions could you just go back to the previous slide yes maybe I'm missing something but you you tell me it's a mismatching digest value but you don't specify whether it was in the representation digest or the content digest but you do tell me the algorithm but the algorithm is in the digest header it would seem like it would be the other way around that you'd want to tell me which digest it was and I you don't need to tell me which algorithm the it's a it's a good point that we could probably also include information whether it was the content or representation digest it's a pretty good one the motivation behind the algorithm was that in uh each digest header field can contain multiple different algorithms so you can provide sh 256 as well as Sha 512 and then the server can report which of those digest identified by the algorithm was not matching okay but we would probably also benefit from um a field for either content or presentation okay and and so the only other question I have is um I mean problem types are an I on a registry so um is the is the intent of writing or the desire to write an RFC here basically just to formalize a description of those parameter those additional parameters that are in the problem type yes okay cool thank you thank you Lucas yeah I as a co-author here I don't have much more to add than Mar already spoke to um but I I will um so with the signatures draft in particular I wasn't sure how much this this working group was aware of that work um so so that's an open question I've got I spoke to just Rich one of the the co-authors of the sickness was draft earlier in the week uh before I got ill um and Maris and I had spoken to him off list as well um effectively what you you have is that that this represent uh these Integrity digests are good at protecting message content but those head of fields themselves that carry the the digests could be manipulated so if you did have something in the middle in the path it could change these things the signatures draft explains how you can protect HTTP metadata so that the header Fields themselves and sign them and have different parties adding them or changing them on all sorts of clever stuff both of the drafts kind of take the approach of creating these building blocks uh and being unspecific about what to do when validation failures happen and so the discussion that we were having is whether we we do this as kind of a joint effort of defining some lowlevel building blocks for the kinds of failures that are likely to happen for people we don't specify what they do with those but that they don't have to go and reinvent the wheel and think through exactly the kind of question you just said darl which is I'll do this oh no didn't quite work because now my deployment Works slightly different to somebody else um I don't think it's that much work um whether we should do the work is is kind of another open question I have um but uh it would help in in some of the scenarios we're having in HTTP on the resumable uploads work um and so you know maybe the HTP working group is better place for doing that but I kind of get the sense that the people who are are working on signatures uh and digest and using those working at like an application Level way above either of these working groups they're going at specifying you know uh open API based Banking workflows and stuff like that so trying to effectively get the best venue to encourage those to participate and and give us some feedback if these standardized problems are useful I think that's what we'd be looking for well and this is the working group that that did the HP problem is at least right so registering problems that correspond to HP problems seems like a reasonable location to do here uh just a quick poll of the room in online do people feel have any strong opinions yes no not opinion yet on whether we should adopt this cage match with the HTTP chairs to see who gets to adopt it yeah Sean's okay um Marius and Lucas uh I think we can make it we can make a call for adoption on the mailing list this seems like a natural fit here but like you guys were pointing out it also seems like it could fit in the HTTP group although maybe a little less naturally um if it's okay if you're fine with HTTP API then we'll make a call for adoption and encourage people to read the doc it's pretty short it's just a few semantics on adding a new entry to the registry yeah Lucas I I think that'd be reasonable for sure um I would I would like us to consider whether signatur should be in scope for inclusion or not and when I spoke to Justin um earlier in the week like I said I wasn't sure if those group is familiar with that work either and that maybe what might help is is if you know uh Justin could come and give his his regular short spiel about what that the that protocol does you know um which might help answer the question or even if we we don't need that ahead of of adopting something we could do it next time so make sure people really understand what we're trying to do yeah I think that would be really good if we could get either you know either app Point well a pointer to the doc on the on our mailing list and also uh consider having him present at the next time in in Dublin um to talk and whether it's you know one small whether it's two small documents or one slightly less small document I don't think it matters very much but uh yeah okay so we will do a call for adoption starting next week um and I encourage people on the list to read it uh anybody opposed Austin go ahead oh yeah um not an opposition by any means quite the opposite uh this is it seems very well thought out and I it makes me wonder if there might be um if maybe after this as like if this is an experiment sort of if there might be opportunities for um taking this idea to other HTTP um you know failure modes like uh you know if I make the wrong type of post request with an unexpected content type or something um that might actually have a standard header but there's some other things in HTTP that don't have standard ways of reporting this is exactly what went wrong and maybe that would also be in scope for the working group here yeah uh I we can talk to Franchesca because they're both in her area and C but sure I'm not I'm not opposed I mean we've got a lot of documents coming out of the pipeline so let's put some more stuff in okay thank you Marius yes thank you thank you as well I would reshare my my slides but the slide says aob on it so I'm not going to bother um which leaves us to does anybody have any other business nobody in the room nobody in the chat all right uh thank you uh we'll probably request next time the same hour um we'll see you know Jonathan can talk to Phil uh thank you all very much for coming enjoy the rest of the week and uh see you online and in Dublin and everybody say happy birthday to my co-chair because it was his birthday Saturday singing is optional okay yes yes definitely I'm gonna go have a beer now good night take care bye bye thank you everybody for
